commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=d643f3211c015a5b6fc395ddba52ad93e86ccbb2)
commands.review_summary:
Adds a `chat.message.get` Gateway protocol/server method, transcript lookup helpers, Control UI sidebar upgrade plumbing, focused tests, and docs for reading full content after truncated WebChat history loads.

PR surface: Source +531, Tests +479, Docs +4. Total +1014 across 29 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main truncates `chat.history` text through display projection and the sidebar expand button passes only the already-loaded markdown. I did not run a live browser reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The blocker is maintainer scope/API judgment plus rebase cleanup, not a narrow automated repair.

target.title: [codex] fix webchat full-message reader for truncated history
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Control UI could only open the already-loaded truncated assistant text in the side reader, so long replies still felt like message loss.
- Solution: add a bounded `chat.message.get` RPC that returns one display-normalized transcript entry by `sessionKey` + transcript `messageId`, then let the side reader upgrade truncated assistant previews on demand.
- What changed: gateway protocol/schema, transcript lookup helper, WebChat sidebar metadata flow, assistant sidebar affordances, visibility checks, focused gateway/UI/docs coverage.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): the default `chat.history` payload cap, transcript storage shape, inline chat bubble size policy, or ambiguous tool-card full-message upgrades.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [x] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #84651
- Related #53242
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Motivation

- WebChat already had a sidebar affordance, but when history projection truncated the loaded assistant text the sidebar repeated the same truncated preview. That made long assistant replies look unrecoverable after refresh.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior addressed: Control UI can now open the full display-normalized content for truncated assistant replies without raising the default `chat.history` payload cap.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout plus Blacksmith Testbox for the changed core/docs surface.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/chat/grouped-render.test.ts src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat-b.test.ts src/gateway/session-utils.fs.test.ts ui/src/ui/views/chat.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-sidebar-full-message.test.ts packages/gateway-protocol/src/index.test.ts`; `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main`; `pnpm check:changed` via Testbox `tbx_01ksxp7tfg583cpn37naza9tny` / Actions run `26698701263`.
- Evidence after fix: Focused gateway/protocol/UI Vitest coverage passed locally; autoreview reported no accepted/actionable findings; Testbox `pnpm check:changed` completed with exit code 0 on the rebased head `04a4c9644c75f86fb00418c4ff4f2bb102d268d8`; after adding generated Swift protocol models, `pnpm protocol:check` and `pnpm test:bundled` passed locally on head `fd8030ded35b73dc4c6e579dba548eca70d3fc03`.
- Observed result after fix: Truncated assistant history rows stay lightweight in the main thread, while opening the side reader fetches the full stored transcript entry when that id is still visible under the same history filters; oversized, hidden, or missing rows return an explicit unavailable state.
- What was not tested: I did not run a manual browser screenshot/video capture against a live localhost Control UI session in this turn; tool-card full-message upgrades are intentionally out of scope because multiple rendered tool cards can share the same transcript id.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: The assistant sidebar open action in `ui/src/ui/chat/grouped-render.ts` only reused the currently loaded `markdown`, while `chat.history` intentionally bounded that field for UI stability.
- Missing detection / guardrail: There was no stable per-message RPC for the UI to ask for one full transcript entry by identity.
- Contributing context (if known): `chat.history` already carried transcript ids in `__openclaw`, but the UI was not using them for a targeted follow-up read.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [ ] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat-b.test.ts`, `src/gateway/session-utils.fs.test.ts`, `ui/src/ui/chat/grouped-render.test.ts`, `ui/src/ui/app-sidebar-full-message.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: truncated assistant history previews upgrade to full content through `chat.message.get`, invisible pre-session rows are rejected by the same history filters, mirrored message-tool replies are not upgraded, and oversized transcript rows return an honest unavailable reason.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the regression spans protocol, transcript lookup, and UI affordance plumbing, so a seam-level gateway test plus focused UI interaction tests catches it without needing a full browser lane.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): existing `chat.history` truncation tests already prove the bounded history half of the behavior.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- Control UI side reader can fetch full assistant content on demand when the visible history preview was truncated.
- If the stored transcript row is oversized or otherwise unavailable, the side reader now says so explicitly instead of silently repeating the truncated preview.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[assistant history preview truncated] -> [open sidebar] -> [same truncated preview]

After:
[assistant history preview truncated] -> [open sidebar] -> [chat.message.get by messageId] -> [full content or explicit unavailable state]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: `chat.message.get` is read-only, uses the existing `operator.read` scope, stays inside the session/transcript ownership and visibility boundary, and returns structured unavailable reasons instead of widening history payloads.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS
- Runtime/container: local source checkout
- Model/provider: not model-specific; transcript/gateway/UI behavior
- Integration/channel (if any): Control UI / WebChat
- Relevant config (redacted): default bounded `chat.history` behavior with focused test harness config

### Steps

1. Seed a session transcript with a long assistant message whose `chat.history` projection is truncated.
2. Load history, capture the truncated preview, then request `chat.message.get` for the same transcript `messageId`.
3. Open the assistant sidebar affordance and verify the sidebar payload carries the full-message request metadata.

### Expected

- `chat.history` stays bounded.
- `chat.message.get` returns the full display-normalized entry when available.
- Oversized rows report an explicit unavailable reason.

### Actual

- Matches expected after the patch.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What I personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Ran focused gateway/protocol/UI Vitest coverage for the new RPC, sidebar metadata flow, visibility filtering, oversized transcript lookup, and raw-sidebar behavior.
- Ran autoreview until clean and `pnpm check:changed` in Blacksmith Testbox after the final rebase.
